items.myth: Saving only the trained model (e.g. RandomForest) is enough for deployment.
items.reality: The fitted preprocessing steps—imputer statistics, encoder categories, scaler means—must ship with the estimator inside one Pipeline artifact or serving will apply wrong or missing transforms.
items.example: Loading `clf.joblib` without the ColumnTransformer means production feeds raw strings and NaN into a forest trained on one-hot encoded, imputed arrays.

items.myth: pandas cleaning and sklearn pipelines are interchangeable ways to do the same work.
items.reality: pandas excels at exploratory, stateless hygiene on DataFrames; sklearn Pipeline enforces fit/transform discipline and serializes learnable parameters. Production needs both roles assigned deliberately.
items.example: A one-off `df.fillna(0)` in a notebook is not equivalent to a train-fitted SimpleImputer inside Pipeline—test rows never saw the notebook cell in the API.

items.myth: ColumnTransformer automatically knows which columns are numeric or categorical.
items.reality: You must pass explicit column lists or selectors; wrong assignments send strings through StandardScaler or numerics through OneHotEncoder.
items.example: Passing `age` as categorical one-hot and `zip_code` as numeric scaled treats postal codes as continuous magnitudes.
